sutta: once the exalted 1 was dwelling in the gable hall in maha vana near vaisali there the exalted 1 addressed the monk saying monks yes lord they replied and the exalted 1 said hold it true monks that the householder of asali is possessed of 8 wonderful and marvelous qualities that spoke the exalted 1 and thereafter rising from his seat the welfare entered the dwelling now a certain monk robbing himself early in the morning taking bowl and clone went to the house of ugga the householder and there sat down on a seat made ready for him [Music] and veysali approached that monk saluted him and sat down at 1 side then said that monk to ooga it has been declared by the exalted 1 that you householder are possessed of 8 marvelous and wonderful qualities what are those 8 marvelous and wonderful qualities householder of which you are declared by the exalted 1 to be possessed and uh said no reverend sir i know not of what 8 marvelous and wonderful qualities i am declared by the exalted 1 to be possessed but as to the 8 marvelous and wonderful qualities found in me listen lend an ear and i will tell you very well householder replied the monk and ooga said when i first saw the exalted 1 even a long way of my heart sir at the mere sight of him became tranquil this is the first marvelous and wonderful quality found in me then with tranquil heart i drew near and reverence the exalted 1 and to me he preached a graduated discourse that is to say on arms giving on virtue on heaven he set forth the peril the folly the depravity of lusts and the advantages of renunciation and when the exalted 1 knew that my heart was clear malleable free from hindrance uplifted lucid then he revealed to me the dhamma teaching to which buddhas alone have won namely bukka is arising its ending and the way to its ending just as a clean cloth free of all stain will die perfectly even so as i said there there are roles within me the spotless stainless dharma site that whatsoever is conditioned by coming to be all check to ending i sir saw dhamma possess dharma found dhamma penetrated dhamma overcame doubt surpassed uncertainty possessed without another's aid perfect confidence in the word of the teacher and there and then took refuge in the buddha in dhamma and in the sangha and embrace the 5 rules of training in the holy life this sir is the second quality [Music] now sir i had 4 wives young girls and i went and spoke to them thus sisters i have embraced the 5 rules of training in the holy life who wishes may enjoy the wealth of this place all may do deeds of merit or may go to her own relations and family or is there some man you desire to whom i may give you and when i had stopped speaking the elder's wife said to me sir give me to such and such a man then i had that man sent forth and taking my wife by the left hand and holding the water pot in my right i cleansed that man by an act of dedication yet i was not aware and discomforted at parting with my wife this is the third quality moreover sir there is wealth in my family but among the good and lovely in character it is shared impartially this is the fourth quality and when i read a point amongst her i serve him respectfully and not without deference that is without respect this sir is the fifth quality hipster the reverend monk preached dhamma to me i listened attentively and not listlessly if he please not dhamma to me i preach dharma to him this sir is the sixth quality furthermore sir it is not uncommon for devas to come and declare to me perfectly o householder is dhamma proclaimed by the exalted 1 when they speak to us i reply you devas whether you declare so or not dhamma is perfectly proclaimed by the exalted 1 yes i feel no elation because of such thoughts as it is to me these devas come i am the man who talks to devas this sir is the seventh quality of those 5 lower factors declared by the exalted 1 i see no remnant whatsoever within me this sir is the eighth marvelous and wonderful quality found in me these are the 8 marvelous and wonderful qualities found in me but i do not know of 1 aid i am declared by the exalted 1 to be possessed then that monk after taking arms at ugga's house roars on his seat and departed and when he had eaten his meal after his arms round he went to the exalted 1 and saluting him stood at 1 side so standing he told the exalted 1 of all his conversations with ooga the householder of the asali and the exalted 1 said well done well done monk as ugga the householder of asali in explaining the matter rightly should explain even so monks he is declared by me to be possessed of those 8 marvelous and wonderful qualities moreover monk wrote it true that the householder of isali is so possessed that's the end of the sutta
commentary: it's quite an interesting suta in the days of the buddha you had such good laypersons like because of past life the moment he saw the buddha his heart became tranquil the last quality the uga says is that those 5 lower factors are no more in him in other words was an anagami non-returner third fruition person who has perfect sila and samadhi and you notice when the monk came to see ooga it is stated here and ooga of isali approached that monk saluted him and sat down at 1 side in other words this lay person even though he's a third fruition person and anagamin he pays respect to the monk he does not think no i am an arya i'm higher than the monk i don't need to pay respect because during the buddha's days they took refuge with the bhikkhu sangha and because the monk was the rope even though he is undergarming he still pays respect the word saluted that means paid homage to the monk and [Music] he noticed that after he understood the dhamma he wanted to practice the spiritual path and keep the 5 precepts he asked he told his 4 young wives that he wanted to practice the holy life and 1 of them wanted to marry another man and he just gave away his wife and then he says that he has wealth in his family but those who are of good character he shares with them not just like his brother he shares with them impartially that means he gave wealth to whatever a virtuous person needed it and then he when he serves a manga he serves him respectfully and then if the monk teaches dharma he listens very attentively the monk comes to his house and does not preach dharma to him then he preaches dharma to the monk he's so capable and devas used to come and talk to him [Music] so that those are the wonderful qualities of the uga [Music] the next uttar i don't know what they can finish once the exalted 1 was dwelling at hatigama among the bhajans there he addressed the monks and said holy true monks that ooga the householder of hatigama is possessed of 8 marvelous and wonderful qualities and when the welfare had thus broken he entered the dwelling then as in the preceding suta among went and asked about those qualities and then said ooga the householder of hatigama when sir i was spotting in naga glade i saw the exalted 1 a good way of at the sight of him my mind became tranquil and my drunkenness vanished this is the first marvelous and wonderful quality the second third fourth and fifth qualities are as in the previous suta it is nothing wonderful for devas to come and tell me when i have invited the sangha that such a 1 is free both ways then he is wisdom free that he is a body witness then he is a view attainer that is faith free that he is a dhamma follower that he is a faithful that he is virtuous and lovely in character or that such a 1 is wicked and evil in character but sir while i read on the sangha i had no such thoughts as to him i will give little to him i will give much rather sir i give impartially this is the sixth quality the seventh is the same as in the previous sutta moreover sir if i should die before the exalted 1 that would not that would not be very wonderful but that the exalted 1 should declare of me that there is no factor flattered by which the householder of hati gama shall come again to this world this is the eighth marvelous and wonderful quality found in me indeed sir these are the 8 by no amount of 1 8 i am declared by the exalted 1 to be possessed then as in the proceedings the man goes and tells the exalted 1 of this conversation who declares that these are just those which he referred that's the end of the suta so this suta is a little similar to the previous 1 but there are few qualities of this householder which is quite wonderful the first 1 he was actually drunk he was enjoying himself in the in the in the gardens and the botanical gardens and he was drunk already but when he saw the buddha his mind became tranquil and his drunkenness vanished this is quite a wonderful thing and it shows to me implies that this person had cultivated strong samadhi in his previous life and because of having cultivated strong samadhi in this life when he saw the buddha something reminded him that this person was very familiar to him and his mind became 1 pointed the mind became 1 tomorrow and his drunkenness just vanished and then the other qualities uh that uh the devas used to come and tell him that such as such a person was an arya or an evil person etc and then when he gives to the nuns he does not dinner this manga will give a little that manga will give much he gives impartially to the monks because he thinks he's supporting the sangha he's not supporting a particular manga and then this person is also anagaming so these are the wonderful qualities of this the householder of their other suitors concerning other wonderful persons and we will talk about it in the next next talk [Music] the last 2 sutas we were talking about the layman disciples of the buddha who were anagamis now we come to another 1 suta

if he please not dhamma to me i preach dharma to him
